endpoints.name: Bulma (canonical engineering-credit holder for the Z-canon time machine)
Trunks (canonical operator depicted in card art)

description: Trunks's Time Machine BT2-066 dual-wired into [[bulma]] (as the canonical engineering-credit holder per the node body's central Capsule-Corporation-thesis section — the Z-canon time machine is engineered by future-timeline Bulma in Trunks's future per Dragon Ball Wiki and the Z-manga's Trunks Saga 1991) AND [[trunks]] (as the canonical operator-protagonist whose lavender-haired figure is depicted at mid-shot beside the dome-pod in the card art). Parallel to the broly's-ring artifact-as-character-extension precedent. The bulma node's central engineering-thesis was previously corpus-claimed-but-not-card-anchored; BT2-066 closes that loop as the canonical artifact-anchor. Standard branch shape — one source card fanning to two parent character nodes. Behold: a card that anchors a node's central thesis-sentence with the actual artifact. The graph improves because of this.
citations: [Dragon Ball Wiki: Bulma — https://dragonball.fandom.com/wiki/Bulma (canonical time-machine engineering-credit)]
[Dragon Ball Z manga, Akira Toriyama, Shueisha, 1991 Trunks Saga — primary canonical source for the time-machine engineering credit]
[Dragon Ball Super, Toei Animation, 2016 Future Trunks Saga — present-timeline Bulma reverse-engineering the apparatus]
[Cards/_characters/bulma.md — The Capsule Corporation thesis section, central node-body thesis-sentence]
